vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-540: Remove convoy abstraction, inline parallel review directly into review-agent

plan.title: Remove convoy abstraction, inline parallel review directly into review-agent
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-6

narratives.Problem: The convoy abstraction (src/lib/convoy.ts, convoy-templates, CLI commands, dashboard panel, convoy:* work types) exists solely to run a fixed set of 4 agents in a fixed order for code review. The fleet-runner use case it was built for was never shipped. It's pure overhead — review-agent already knows which reviewers it needs.
narratives.Proposal: Inline the parallel-review runner directly into review-agent.ts, delete all convoy machinery, rename convoy:* work types to review:* (with auto-migration of user overrides), and add a user-configurable specialists.review_agents[] list in config.yaml. Refactor first, delete second — every bead leaves the tree green.

narrative.Action: Replace startConvoy('code-review', ctx) + waitForConvoy(id) + parseConvoySynthesis(dir) in src/lib/cloister/review-agent.ts with a local runParallelReview() helper that spawns N reviewer tmux sessions in parallel, polls for completion, runs synthesis, and parses synthesis.md output. Hardcode the default 3 reviewers (correctness, security, performance) + synthesis for this bead — configurability is added in a later bead. Write outputs to .pan/review/<reviewId>/ (not .pan/convoy/). Remove all imports from src/lib/convoy.ts in review-agent.ts. Convoy files stay on disk (deleted in later beads) — nothing else breaks.

subItems.title: src/lib/cloister/review-agent.ts has no imports from src/lib/convoy.ts or src/lib/convoy-templates.ts

A new local helper (e.g. runParallelReview) spawns 3 reviewer tmux sessions in parallel (correctness, security, performance) and polls for their completion using the same cadence as the old waitForConvoy
After all 3 reviewers complete, a synthesis agent runs and produces synthesis.md with REVIEW_RESULT / FILES_REVIEWED / SECURITY_ISSUES / PERFORMANCE_ISSUES / NOTES markers

Synthesis rules enforced: any reviewer CHANGES_REQUESTED ⇒ overall CHANGES_REQUESTED; security issues always surfaced; duplicate findings (same file + same concern) collapsed; findings attributed to source reviewer
Reviewer output files are written to .pan/review/<reviewId>/ (not .pan/convoy/<id>/)
End-to-end review flow works on a sample PR: spawns 3 reviewers, runs synthesis, returns a parsed ReviewResult with correct status
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test all pass

items.title: Rename convoy:* work types to review:* with auto-migration

narrative.Action: Rename the 5 work-type keys across lib and frontend: convoy:correctness-reviewer → review:correctness, convoy:security-reviewer → review:security, convoy:performance-reviewer → review:performance, convoy:requirements-reviewer → review:requirements, convoy:synthesis-agent → review:synthesis. Update src/lib/work-types.ts, src/lib/smart-model-selector.ts, src/lib/settings-api.ts, src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Settings/types.ts (WorkTypeId union), src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Settings/SettingsPage.tsx (rename section header from 'Convoy Reviewers' to 'Review Agents'). In settings-api.ts, add a one-time auto-migration shim: when loading persisted model overrides, silently rewrite any surviving convoy:* keys to their review:* equivalents so existing user settings keep working without user action.

subItems.title: src/lib/work-types.ts defines review:correctness, review:security, review:performance, review:requirements, review:synthesis and no convoy:* entries
src/lib/smart-model-selector.ts routes the 5 review:* work types to their mapped default models

src/lib/settings-api.ts auto-migrates persisted convoy:* keys to review:* on load; a user with pre-existing convoy:security-reviewer override sees it preserved under review:security with no manual action
Settings/types.ts WorkTypeId union contains review:* keys and no convoy:* keys
Settings UI renders a 'Review Agents' section with the 5 review:* cards; no 'Convoy Reviewers' string remains in the frontend
review-agent.ts (from the previous bead) resolves reviewer model IDs via the new review:* keys
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test all pass

items.title: Add specialists.review_agents[] config schema and wire into review-agent

narrative.Action: Extend SpecialistsConfig in src/lib/cloister/config.ts with a new optional field review_agents?: ReviewAgentConfig[]. ReviewAgentConfig has { name: string; model: ModelId; focus?: ReviewFocus[]; enabled?: boolean (default true) }. Parse it from projects.yaml/config.yaml. Update the runParallelReview helper in review-agent.ts to read this list when present (honoring enabled=false), falling back to the default 3 (correctness, security, performance) when absent. Per-agent model overrides take precedence over the review:<name> work-type default. Pass agent.focus (when set) into the reviewer prompt so the agent knows its focus areas.

subItems.title: src/lib/cloister/config.ts exports ReviewAgentConfig type and SpecialistsConfig.review_agents?: ReviewAgentConfig[]
projects.yaml parser accepts specialists.review_agents list with name/model/focus/enabled fields
When specialists.review_agents is absent, review-agent runs the default 3 reviewers (correctness, security, performance) — existing behavior is preserved
When specialists.review_agents is present, review-agent spawns exactly the enabled agents from that list (skipping enabled: false entries)
Per-agent model override takes precedence over the review:<name> work-type default
When focus[] is set on an agent, the focus areas are included in the reviewer prompt sent to that agent
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test all pass

items.title: Delete CLI convoy commands and unregister them

narrative.Action: Delete src/cli/commands/convoy/{index,start,stop,list,status}.ts entirely. Remove the registerConvoyCommands import and call from src/cli/index.ts. `pan convoy …` should exit with 'unknown command'.

subItems.title: src/cli/commands/convoy/ directory no longer exists
src/cli/index.ts has no reference to registerConvoyCommands or any convoy command import
Running `pan convoy start code-review` prints an 'unknown command' error (not a runtime crash)
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test all pass

narrative.Action: Remove the 5 convoy endpoints from src/dashboard/server/routes/metrics.ts (GET /api/convoys, GET /api/convoys/:id, POST /api/convoys/start, POST /api/convoys/:id/stop, GET /api/convoys/:id/output) and drop the imports of startConvoy/stopConvoy/getConvoyStatus/listConvoys at the top of that file. If any other route module imports from src/lib/convoy.ts, clean those too.

subItems.title: src/dashboard/server/routes/metrics.ts has no references to convoy (imports or route handlers)
GET /api/convoys returns 404 (route is gone, not 500)
No other file in src/dashboard/server/routes/ imports from src/lib/convoy.ts
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test all pass

items.title: Delete ConvoyPanel component and useConvoys hook

narrative.Action: Delete src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/ConvoyPanel.tsx and src/dashboard/frontend/src/hooks/useConvoys.ts. Remove any remaining imports from other frontend files — should be zero after the nav/route bead lands. This bead and remove-convoys-nav-route ship as a pair to keep the frontend compiling.

subItems.title: src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/ConvoyPanel.tsx no longer exists
src/dashboard/frontend/src/hooks/useConvoys.ts no longer exists
No file under src/dashboard/frontend/ imports ConvoyPanel or useConvoys
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test all pass; frontend vite build succeeds

items.title: Remove Convoys sidebar nav item and /convoys route

narrative.Action: Remove the { id: 'convoys', label: 'Convoys', icon: Network } entry from NAV_GROUPS in src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Sidebar.tsx and delete the /convoys route from src/dashboard/frontend/src/App.tsx. Clean up the Network icon import if no other nav entry uses it.

subItems.title: Sidebar.tsx NAV_GROUPS has no 'convoys' entry
App.tsx has no /convoys route
Dashboard sidebar no longer renders a Convoys nav item; visiting /convoys hits the app's default/fallback route
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test all pass

items.title: Delete convoy.ts, convoy-templates.ts, YAML templates, and tests

narrative.Action: Delete src/lib/convoy.ts, src/lib/convoy-templates.ts, the src/agents/convoy/ directory (all template YAML files), and tests/lib/convoy.test.ts. This bead must land after all consumers (review-agent, CLI commands, dashboard routes, frontend panel) are gone — at this point nothing should import from either file. Grep case-insensitive for 'convoy' across src/ to confirm zero non-docs references remain.

subItems.title: src/lib/convoy.ts and src/lib/convoy-templates.ts no longer exist
src/agents/convoy/ directory no longer exists
tests/lib/convoy.test.ts no longer exists
Case-insensitive grep for 'convoy' across src/ returns zero matches (docs/ may still have pending updates in a later bead)
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test all pass

items.title: Update documentation to remove convoy references

narrative.Action: Remove the convoy section (if any) from docs/USAGE.md, update docs/INDEX.md if it references convoys, and rewrite docs/CONFIGURATION.md to replace all convoy:* work-type examples with review:* equivalents and document the new specialists.review_agents[] config schema with an example. Grep case-insensitive for 'convoy' across docs/ to confirm no stale references remain (except historical changelog entries, which may stay).

subItems.title: docs/USAGE.md has no convoy section
docs/INDEX.md has no references to convoys
docs/CONFIGURATION.md documents the new specialists.review_agents[] schema with a realistic example showing name/model/focus/enabled fields
docs/CONFIGURATION.md examples use review:* work-type keys (no convoy:* strings remain in current config examples)
Case-insensitive grep for 'convoy' across docs/ returns only intentional historical references (none in current how-to or config sections)
